info.title: Wardnet API
info.description: Self-hosted network privacy gateway — REST API for device, tunnel, routing, DHCP, DNS, and update management.

post.description: Log in with username and password. On success, sets a `wardnet_session` cookie the browser replays on subsequent admin-gated requests. The JSON body also carries the raw `token` and `expires_in_seconds` so non-browser clients (scripts, the `wctl` CLI, third-party integrations) can replay the token via `Authorization: Bearer <token>` without a cookie jar.

200.description: Login successful; session cookie is set

401.description: Invalid credentials

get.description: List every device the daemon has seen on the LAN, enriched with its current DHCP status (reservation, active lease, or external/unknown). Admin only.

200.description: List of devices with DHCP status

401.description: Unauthenticated — session cookie or API key missing/invalid

request_id.description: Request ID for correlation with server logs.

403.description: Forbidden — caller is not an admin

request_id.description: Request ID for correlation with server logs.
Request ID for correlation with server logs.

get.description: Identify the caller by source IP and return their device info, current routing rule, and the list of available tunnels they can route through. Used by the self-service "My Device" page, so no authentication is required — the daemon trusts the source IP of the incoming TCP connection.

200.description: Caller device info and available tunnels

put.description: Let the caller change their own routing rule (direct vs. a specific tunnel). The target device is identified by source IP. Admin-locked devices are rejected with 403 — an admin must lift the lock first. No authentication is required (self-service by IP).

403.description: Device is admin-locked

get.description: Fetch a single device by ID, including its current routing rule and DHCP status. Admin only.

post.description: Create the first admin account as part of the initial setup wizard. Password is hashed with Argon2id before persistence. No authentication required (because by definition no admin exists yet), but returns 409 Conflict if setup has already been completed to prevent this endpoint from being used as an admin-creation backdoor.

get.description: Report whether the initial setup wizard has been completed (i.e. whether any admin account exists). No authentication required — the web UI calls this before rendering to decide whether to redirect the user to the setup page.
Return the most recent warnings and errors captured by the in-memory error notifier ring buffer (currently the last 15 entries). Powers the dashboard's "recent issues" panel. Admin only.

AllowlistEntry.description: An allowlist entry that overrides blocklist matches.

request_id.description: Request ID for correlation with server logs.

ApiErrorEntry.description:
API-layer mirror of [`wardnetd_services::logging::error_notifier::ErrorEntry`] that
carries an `OpenAPI` schema. The service-layer type lives in a crate that does
not depend on `utoipa`; duplicating the small struct here keeps the schema
boundary aligned with the HTTP API without leaking the docs dependency into
the services crate.

Blocklist.description: A URL-sourced domain blocklist for ad blocking.

last_error.description:
Error message from the most recent failed download/parse, if any.
Cleared on a successful refresh. Surfaced in the Ad Blocking UI so
users can diagnose blocklist issues without inspecting daemon logs.

last_error_at.description: Timestamp of the most recent failed download/parse.

CountryInfo.description: A country available from a VPN provider.

available_tunnels.description: Available tunnels for self-service routing selection.

DeviceType.description: The type/category of a network device.

DeviceWithStatus.description:
A device enriched with its DHCP status for API responses.

Uses `#[serde(flatten)]` so the JSON output includes all `Device` fields
at the top level alongside `dhcp_status`, keeping the response
backwards-compatible for consumers that ignore unknown fields.

DhcpConfig.description:
DHCP pool configuration.

Wardnet IS the gateway and DNS server — no need for users to configure those.
The daemon auto-detects its own LAN IP and advertises itself as gateway (option 3)
and DNS server (option 6) to clients. Option 3 includes the router IP as secondary
entry for automatic failover on supported clients.

gateway_ip.description:
Wardnet's own LAN IP — auto-detected from the LAN interface.
Advertised as gateway (option 3) and DNS server (option 6).

router_ip.description: Fallback router — the real router's IP, included as secondary in option 3.

DhcpLease.description: An active or historical DHCP lease.

DhcpLeaseStatus.description: The current status of a DHCP lease.

DhcpStatus.description: Whether a device's IP is managed by the wardnet DHCP server.

DnsConfig.description:
Top-level DNS server configuration.

Persisted as individual keys in the `system_config` KV table,
following the same pattern as [`crate::dhcp::DhcpConfig`].

DnsProtocol.description: Transport protocol for upstream DNS communication.

InfoResponse.description:
Response from the public info endpoint.

Returns basic server information without requiring authentication.
Used by the web UI's connection status widget.

InstallHandle.description: Handle returned when an install is kicked off.

oneOf.description: Resolving the manifest for the target channel.
Verifying SHA-256 and (if enabled) minisign signature.
Extracting and staging the binary on disk.
Swap completed, daemon will restart now.
The new binary is running; clearing pending marker.
Install failed — message contains the reason.

InstallPhase.description: Phase of an in-flight update install.

InstallUpdateRequest.description:
Request body for POST /api/update/install.

If `version` is omitted, installs the latest known version for the current
channel. The operation is idempotent — calling twice while one is in
flight returns the same handle.

Job.description: Snapshot of a job, returned by `GET /api/jobs/:id`.

percentage_done.description: Completion percentage, 0..=100. Jumps to 100 when the job terminates.

JobDispatchedResponse.description:
Response body for a handler that dispatches a background job instead of
doing the work inline. The HTTP status code is `202 Accepted`.

JobKind.description:
Discriminator for what kind of work a job represents. The kind is fixed
at job creation time; new kinds get new variants here.

JobStatus.description:
Lifecycle state of a [`Job`]. Jobs start as `Pending`, move to `Running`
when execution begins, and end in one of the two terminal states.

credentials.description: Credentials for authenticating with the provider.

filter.description: Optional filters for the server list.

servers.description: List of available servers from the provider.

LoginResponse.description:
Login response body.

`token` is the same opaque value written into the `wardnet_session` cookie;
non-browser clients (e.g. scripts that don't maintain a cookie jar) can
replay it on admin-gated requests via the `Authorization: Bearer <token>`
header. `expires_in_seconds` is the token's remaining lifetime measured
from the moment this response was generated.

ProviderAuthMethod.description: Supported authentication methods for a VPN provider.

ProviderCredentials.description: Credentials submitted by the admin for provider operations.

ProviderInfo.description: Metadata about a registered VPN provider.

credentials_hint.description: Hint text explaining where to find credentials (shown in the UI).

icon_url.description: URL to the provider's icon/logo for UI display.

RoutingTarget.description: Where traffic for a device should be routed.

ServerInfo.description: Information about a single VPN server.

credentials.description: Credentials for authenticating with the provider.

hostname.description:
Direct server hostname for dedicated IP or manual server selection.
Bypasses server listing -- resolves directly by hostname.
Accepts short form (`pt131`) or full (`pt131.nordvpn.com`).
Takes precedence over `server_id` when both are set.

label.description: Optional label override; defaults to provider-generated label.

server_id.description: If set, use this specific server ID instead of auto-selecting.

Tunnel.description: A `WireGuard` tunnel configuration and its live state.

TunnelStatus.description: The current status of a `WireGuard` tunnel.

TunnelSummary.description: Minimal tunnel info exposed to self-service users for routing selection.

UpdateChannel.description:
Update release channel.

`Stable` tracks only full semver releases (e.g. `0.1.2`). `Beta` also
considers pre-release builds. `Beta` is v2 scope; the v1 daemon still
accepts the value but the runner only checks `Stable`.

admin_locked.description: Whether to lock routing changes for this device.

oneOf.description: Routing target to set for this device (admin bypasses lock check).

UpdateHistoryEntry.description: A single entry in the persistent update history.

UpdateHistoryStatus.description: Final status of a historical update attempt.

UpdateStatus.description: Snapshot of the update subsystem's current state.

last_check_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the last successful check, if any.

last_install_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the most recent install attempt.

latest_version.description: Latest version known for the active channel (if any check has run).

pending_version.description: Version the in-flight install is targeting (if any).

rollback_available.description: Whether a `.old` binary is present that could be rolled back to.

update_available.description: Whether a newer version than the running one is available.

port.description: Port (default: 53 for UDP/TCP, 853 for TLS, 443 for HTTPS).

UpstreamDnsRequest.description: Upstream DNS server in API requests.

credentials.description: Credentials to validate against the provider.

tags.name: dhcp
tags.description: DHCP server configuration, leases, and reservations
